title: Weight the Odds
html:
The bite was worse by midday.

I noticed it around the 18-hour mark. Dae-Jung was sitting against the rock face with his forearm across his knee, and the rope bandage had soaked through in a pattern that was not consistent with a wound that was closing. Dark at the center, lighter at the edge, the kind of spread that moves outward from a point rather than drying in place. He was not favoring it. He was not mentioning it. He was watching the camp with the same focused attention as before, tracking the 4 unnamed survivors, checking the sight lines at intervals. Doing his job.

I walked over and crouched beside him.

"Let me see it."

He looked at me.

"It is fine."

"I did not ask if it was fine."

A beat. He unwound the rope from his forearm.

The wound had spread since the morning. Not dramatically, not visibly infected in the way infections look in the first stage, but the dark staining at the edge of the bite was not blood. It was something else. It had a faint iridescence in the midday light, the kind you see in oil on water, and the skin around the bite margin was slightly raised in a way that pressure bandages do not cause.

I looked at it for a moment.

'Use it,' I thought. 'You have it.'

I had not used Weight the Odds yet. I had read the description four times and thought about the 30-second window and the 60-minute cooldown and decided each time that the situation did not yet justify burning the first use. This situation justified it.

I activated the ability and looked at Dae-Jung's forearm.

[object Object]

【"Weight the Odds -- Active"】

【"Target: Dae-Jung / Wound (Bite, Nocturnal Hunter)"】

【"Analysis window: 30 seconds"】

【"Finding: Venom present. Classification: Slow-acting paralytic, Tier 2. Onset: 8-12 hours post-bite. Current elapsed: approx 9 hours."】

【"Exploitable variables: Onset delayed by physical activity (increases metabolism, slows neurological uptake). Current trajectory without intervention: partial paralysis of affected limb within 3 hours."】

【"Intervention window: Open. Purification tablets contain a compound effective against Tier 1-2 venoms when applied topically. Efficacy: 60-70% at current stage."】

【"No further variables identified."】

[object Object]

30 seconds. I looked at the information and filed it in the order of its urgency.

Partial paralysis of his right arm in 3 hours. Purification tablets applied topically with 60-70% efficacy. Physical activity slowing the uptake.

I stood and pulled a tablet from my inventory. Three left. Two after this.

"I need to apply this to the wound," I said. "It will probably work."

He looked at the tablet. He looked at my face.

"Probably."

"60 to 70 percent."

He thought about that for approximately one second.

"Do it."

I broke the tablet and pressed the compound directly into the bite margin. It was not a pleasant process. Dae-Jung made a sound once, short and closed-mouthed, and then was quiet. The iridescent staining at the wound edge reacted to the compound -- I watched it pull back slightly from the margin, not disappearing but retreating, the way a spilled liquid responds to a barrier.

"Keep moving," I said. "Walk around the camp. Do not sit still."

He rewrapped the forearm and stood without further comment. He walked to the far end of the clearing and back and then began a patrol loop around the camp perimeter, which was the most activity available in the space. I watched the wound margin on his arm from a distance. The retreat held.

Maya was beside me. I had not heard her approach.

"Venom," she said. Not a question.

"Yes."

"Is it in the system bestiary? The creature that bit him?"

I had not checked whether a bestiary existed. I pulled up the HUD and looked. There was a tab I had not opened, filed under Encounters.

The nocturnal hunter was listed. The system name was Shade-Rank: Prowler. The entry was sparse: photosensitive, pack-hunting, Tier 2 threat, venom classified as paralytic. Below the entry was a note in the same neutral-cheerful system font: 'More information available upon direct encounter.'

"There is a bestiary," I said. "We have a partial entry on the Prowler. Not enough."

"What would a direct encounter give us?"

"More information."

She looked at the camp. At Dae-Jung walking his circuit.

"That seems like a bad trade."

"Probably," I said.

[object Object]

【"Tutorial Chapter 1: Hour 18 Update"】

【"Participants Remaining: 74 / 103"】

【"Tip: Information is also a resource. Some of it costs more than others."】

[object Object]

74 now. Down 5 in 3 hours. The attrition was not slowing.

I looked at the 11 people at the camp. Food for maybe one more partial meal at current shares. Water covered for today if we rationed it. The camp position was good but static. Staying here for 30 more hours with 11 people and diminishing resources was a plan that got worse as it continued.

Pita came back from the northwest sight line at the same moment the system update cleared.

"There is something I want to show you," she said.

She had the flat economy of someone who has decided to share information and is not going to perform it. I followed her to the treeline without asking the others to wait.

About 40 meters into the trees she stopped and pointed at the ground.

A cache marker. Not a Tier 1 root-and-moss placement like Soo-Yun had found. This was a flat stone with a deliberate arrangement of smaller stones on its surface in a pattern that was not natural. Not the system's cache placement. Something else.

"Someone placed this," I said.

"Yes. Today. The ground compression around the stone is fresh."

I crouched and looked at it. The pattern on the stone was not decorative. It was a direction marker. Pointing northeast.

"How far have you ranged northeast?" I asked.

"Not yet. I wanted you to see this first."

I looked at the marker and then at the trees to the northeast. The forest in that direction ran denser for the first 20 meters and then the canopy seemed higher, which usually meant older growth, which usually meant different ground-level conditions. Possibly more caches. Possibly the person who left the marker.

The two other Arbiters were somewhere in this forest.

I did not say that out loud.

"We move northeast after midday," I said. "All of us."

Pita looked at the marker, then at me.

"The group will slow us down."

"Yes."

She accepted the trade the same way she accepted everything. With the minimal expression of someone who has already thought it through and is confirming that you have too.

We went back to the camp.

The unnamed survivors needed names, or at least functions, before we moved. I had been filing them as a unit but a group that moves through uncertain terrain needs individual accountability. I pulled them into a conversation at the rock face over what remained of the morning.

Their names: Tomasz, Blessing, Hana, and Fen. Tomasz was the one with the bandaged forearm that was not Dae-Jung -- a long gash from a branch, not a bite, clean and closing. Blessing had a bruised shoulder and moved her left arm carefully. Hana and Fen were uninjured, had stayed together since the sealed room, and had the close-shouldered posture of people who have decided that the two of them are enough and are not sure about expanding.

I noted all of this without taking notes. Maya took notes.

"We are moving this afternoon," I told them. "Northeast. Single file on my signal. Stay in pairs. Tell the person beside you if you hear something before you tell me."

Hana looked at Fen. Fen looked at me.

"Why northeast?" Fen asked.

"Trail marker. Possibly another cache."

"Possibly."

"Yes."

Fen absorbed this. He had the look of someone who wanted more certainty and had learned in the last 18 hours that more certainty was not available. He nodded.

Soo-Yun appeared at my left elbow.

"Sir Eric. Renata wants to know if her head is going to be a problem on the walk."

I looked across the camp at Renata, who was watching me and clearly aware that Soo-Yun was relaying the question rather than asking it herself. Which meant Renata had chosen the buffer deliberately.

I walked over to her directly.

"How is your head?"

She held my gaze. Direct, steady. Snow country manners, maybe, or just her own.

"It hurts. It will not stop me."

"If it gets worse you tell me immediately."

"Why?"

"Because a head injury that worsens on exertion is a different category of problem than one that holds steady."

She considered that.

"Understood," she said.

That was the most words I had exchanged with her. I noted that she had needed a direct approach, not a buffer, once the buffer option was not available.

Midday. We ate the last of the food in small portions. Four crackers remained from a packet someone in Dae-Jung's group had been carrying. Distributed across 11 people, they were a gesture rather than a meal.

Pita handed me something before we moved. A piece of bark with a rough sketch on it. The stream, the rock face, the direction marker, the northeast bearing. She had drawn it from memory in under a minute.

"From the warm islands," she said, without being asked.

I looked up.

She was looking at the sketch.

"You were going to need a descriptor," she said. "For wherever I am from. Warm islands is accurate enough."

I filed it. Warm islands.

"Thank you."

"Do not thank me. Just do not get me killed."

She moved to the front of the column without waiting for a response.

We entered the trees at the 20-hour mark. 11 people, two impaired, one with venom in his system that was 60 to 70 percent addressed, one with a head injury that was holding steady, and me with an ability I had used once and 28 hours left on a clock that was not interested in whether we were ready.

The forest swallowed us in about 4 steps. The camp disappeared behind us.

I checked the direction marker in my memory and kept walking.

[object Object]

【"Movement logged: Group northeast"】

【"Note: You are not the only ones moving."】

[object Object]

"I thought: I know."

I kept my eyes on the trees and my hand near the machete and I did not slow down.
